hubSubtitle: Third-party options and education — OS Kitchen does not originate loans or make credit decisions.
referralFeeDisclosure: KitchenOS may receive referral compensation from some partners in the future. Any active referral relationships will be disclosed on this page before launch.
hubDisclosures: OS Kitchen is not a lender, broker, or financial advisor.
Links open third-party websites governed by their own terms and privacy policies.
Revenue figures shown in your dashboard are operational summaries — not lender-certified attestations.
Approval, rates, and repayment terms are determined solely by the financing provider.
Consult qualified legal and accounting advisors before signing any financing agreement.

partners.name: U.S. Small Business Administration — Funding programs
partners.description: Federal small-business loan programs, Lender Match, and disaster assistance resources for restaurant operators in the United States.
partners.disclosure: U.S. government resource — KitchenOS is not affiliated with the SBA.

partners.name: SBA — Restaurant industry guide
partners.description: Planning, licensing, and capital planning guides for food-service businesses evaluating SBA-backed options.

partners.name: SCORE — Free business mentoring
partners.description: Volunteer mentors help operators prepare financial statements and lender conversations before applying for working capital.
partners.disclosure: Nonprofit mentoring network — not a lender.

partners.name: Canada — Business development programs
partners.description: Provincial and federal small-business financing directories for Canadian restaurant operators.

partners.name: British Business Bank — Start Up Loans
partners.description: UK government-backed guidance and loan programs for hospitality businesses.
partners.disclosure: UK government-backed programs — verify eligibility with the provider.

partners.name: OS Kitchen — Signed revenue export
partners.description: Generate a signed JSON summary of trailing gross order revenue from your KitchenOS workspace (Phase 2 beta).
partners.disclosure: Operational revenue summary — not a lending product or credit decision.

partners.name: Pilot — Revenue-based financing (sandbox)
partners.description: Beta sandbox for KitchenOS embedded lender offers. Requires explicit consent before sharing your signed revenue export.
partners.disclosure: Sandbox pilot partner — not a live funding guarantee. Terms and eligibility are set solely by the lender.
partners.offerDisclosure: By continuing you leave OS Kitchen and apply directly with the lender. OS Kitchen does not guarantee approval, rates, or funding timelines.
partners.offerAmountLabel: Illustrative offers from $5k–$250k based on trailing KitchenOS revenue

partners.name: Pilot — Equipment financing (Canada sandbox)
partners.description: Sandbox equipment financing partner for Canadian restaurant operators. Compare terms alongside working-capital options.
partners.disclosure: Sandbox pilot partner for Canada — not a live funding guarantee.
partners.offerDisclosure: Equipment loans are collateralized by assets. OS Kitchen does not guarantee approval or rates.

partners.name: Pilot — Working capital (UK sandbox)
partners.description: UK hospitality working-capital sandbox with multi-offer webhook support for comparison in your referral inbox.
partners.disclosure: UK sandbox partner — verify FCA authorisation and terms with the lender directly.
partners.offerDisclosure: KitchenOS may receive referral compensation. Terms set solely by the lender.
partners.offerAmountLabel: Illustrative offers from £5k–£150k based on trailing revenue

educationTopics.title: Working capital vs. equipment financing
educationTopics.body: Working capital covers payroll gaps and inventory spikes; equipment loans are collateralized by assets. Match the product to the use case before applying.

educationTopics.title: Revenue-based financing (RBF) basics
educationTopics.body: Some POS platforms offer advances repaid via daily sales holdbacks. Compare effective APR, holdback percentage, and personal guarantee requirements.

educationTopics.title: What lenders typically ask for
educationTopics.body: Trailing sales history, bank statements, time in business, and location count. KitchenOS analytics can help you prepare — export attestation is on the Phase 2 roadmap.

educationTopics.title: Connect analytics to your application
educationTopics.body: Use Revenue and Advanced analytics in OS Kitchen to understand gross order volume, cancellation rate, and channel mix before speaking with a lender.
